name: Yam Extended
description: Yam, also known as Prince Sea, Judge River, and often referred to as Yam Extended to emphasize his vast dominion, is a central figure in Canaanite mythology, embodying the primordial forces of the sea and chaos. He is the god of the sea, rivers, and all bodies of water, representing the turbulent and untamed aspects of nature. Born from the union of El, the chief god, and Asherah, the mother goddess, Yam inherited a claim to kingship over the other gods, sparking a cosmic conflict that defines much of the Canaanite mythological cycle. His power lies in his control over the waters, capable of unleashing devastating storms, floods, and droughts. He is depicted as arrogant and ambitious, constantly seeking to usurp the authority of Baal Hadad, the storm god, and establish his dominion over the divine assembly. Yam's palace is located deep within the ocean, a realm of immense power and potential danger. He demands tribute and recognition from the other deities, viewing himself as the rightful ruler of the cosmos. His ultimate defeat by Baal symbolizes the triumph of order over chaos and the establishment of a balanced cosmic order. The myths surrounding Yam serve as a metaphor for the ongoing struggle between stability and instability, and the vital role of the storm god in maintaining equilibrium within the world. He is a powerful and formidable deity, representing the raw, untamed power of the sea.
subtitle: God of the Sea and Chaos

keyMyths.title: Yam's Claim to Kingship
keyMyths.description: Yam demands tribute and kingship over the gods, challenging Baal's authority and sparking a conflict.

keyMyths.title: Baal's Victory Over Yam
keyMyths.description: Baal, with the help of Kothar-wa-Khasis' crafted clubs, defeats Yam in a cosmic battle, establishing order.

keyMyths.title: Yam's Palace in the Sea
keyMyths.description: Yam resides in a magnificent palace beneath the waves, symbolizing his dominion over the aquatic realm.

sources.author: Mark S. Smith
sources.work: The Ugaritic Baal Cycle, Volume I
sources.description: A comprehensive translation and analysis of the Baal Cycle texts.
sources.text: A comprehensive translation and analysis of the Baal Cycle texts.
